verses.text: For twenty-three years Jeremiah has spoken God's word, 'but ye have not hearkened.' His ministry was long and faithful. Persistent preaching to a deaf people is a heavy but God-given task.
For twenty-three years Jeremiah has spoken God's word, 'but ye have not hearkened.' His ministry was long and faithful. Persistent preaching to a deaf people is a heavy but God-given task.
For twenty-three years Jeremiah has spoken God's word, 'but ye have not hearkened.' His ministry was long and faithful. Persistent preaching to a deaf people is a heavy but God-given task.
God sent all His prophets, 'rising early,' calling them to turn, but they would not. His patience was great. Heaven's repeated warnings went unheeded by a stubborn people.
God sent all His prophets, 'rising early,' calling them to turn, but they would not. His patience was great. Heaven's repeated warnings went unheeded by a stubborn people.
God sent all His prophets, 'rising early,' calling them to turn, but they would not. His patience was great. Heaven's repeated warnings went unheeded by a stubborn people.
God sent all His prophets, 'rising early,' calling them to turn, but they would not. His patience was great. Heaven's repeated warnings went unheeded by a stubborn people.
Therefore He will bring Nebuchadnezzar against them, and 'these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.' The term is fixed. God measures even the years of His people's chastening.
Therefore He will bring Nebuchadnezzar against them, and 'these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.' The term is fixed. God measures even the years of His people's chastening.
Therefore He will bring Nebuchadnezzar against them, and 'these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.' The term is fixed. God measures even the years of His people's chastening.
Therefore He will bring Nebuchadnezzar against them, and 'these nations shall serve the king of Babylon seventy years.' The term is fixed. God measures even the years of His people's chastening.
When the seventy years are done, He will punish Babylon and make it 'perpetual desolations.' The instrument is judged too. God uses the proud, then repays their pride.
When the seventy years are done, He will punish Babylon and make it 'perpetual desolations.' The instrument is judged too. God uses the proud, then repays their pride.
When the seventy years are done, He will punish Babylon and make it 'perpetual desolations.' The instrument is judged too. God uses the proud, then repays their pride.
Jeremiah is given 'the wine cup of this fury' to make the nations drink. Judgment is a cup. The wrath of God is poured out on all who provoke Him.
Jeremiah is given 'the wine cup of this fury' to make the nations drink. Judgment is a cup. The wrath of God is poured out on all who provoke Him.
Jeremiah is given 'the wine cup of this fury' to make the nations drink. Judgment is a cup. The wrath of God is poured out on all who provoke Him.
Jerusalem first, then Egypt and the surrounding kings, must all drink the cup. None is exempt. God's people are judged first, then the nations around.

Jerusalem first, then Egypt and the surrounding kings, must all drink the cup. None is exempt. God's people are judged first, then the nations around.

Tyre, Sidon, Arabia, and the kings of the north and 'all the kingdoms of the world' shall drink, and last of all the king of Sheshach. The whole earth is included. No nation stands outside God's reckoning.

They shall drink and fall and rise no more; for 'I begin to bring evil on the city which is called by my name.' Judgment starts at God's house. If His own people are not spared, how shall the wicked escape?

They shall drink and fall and rise no more; for 'I begin to bring evil on the city which is called by my name.' Judgment starts at God's house. If His own people are not spared, how shall the wicked escape?
The LORD shall 'roar from on high' like one treading grapes; the slain shall cover the earth. His wrath is terrible. The Judge of all the earth executes a worldwide reckoning.

The shepherds shall howl, for the LORD has spoiled their pasture and left His covert like a lion. The leaders are undone. When God arises in fury, the mightiest are scattered.
